description:
Model a decommissioned quarry pit and design a floating solar (FPV) array on it in SketchUp using the Skelion plugin.

Instructions:
1. Model a tapered quarry pit:
   - Create a rectangular opening at ground level: 80 meters × 60 meters.
   - Create the pit floor 15 meters below the ground level: 60 meters × 40 meters, centered below the opening.
   - Connect the edges so the walls slope evenly from the top opening to the bottom floor.
2. Create the Water Body:
   - Draw a flat face inside the pit to represent the water surface at a height of 10 meters above the pit floor (which is 5 meters below the ground level).
   - Apply a standard translucent 'Water' material to this surface.
3. Design the Floating Array:
   - Select the water surface face.
   - Open the Skelion plugin and insert solar panels.
   - Set Tilt to 12 degrees.
   - Set Orientation to face South.
   - Ensure there is approximately a 5-meter buffer/setback between the solar array and the 'shoreline' (where the water meets the sloped banks) to allow room for anchoring.
4. Save the completed model to: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\quarry_floating_solar.skp
